sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode sich1238.

notes: Nuosu / Sichuan Yi (ꆇꉙ) — Loloish (Ngwi) language of Sichuan, written in the Yi script. INPUT CONTRACT: the modern standard Yi script is a SYLLABARY of 819 standardised syllabograms (each glyph = one CV syllable with tone), so no letter-level grapheme map exists or is claimed here; THE GRAPHEME INVENTORY IS NOT YET SPECIFIED (graphemes/allophones are empty). A full syllabogram→IPA table, or else romanised input in the official Yi Pinyin (e.g. ⟨bbop⟩), would be required — exactly as `zh` requires pinyin. Nuosu is tonal (four tones, written by tone-diacritic-derived glyph variants). PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode sich1238), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

ancestors.notes: Sino-Tibetan — the classification node this language sits under.

location.notes: Glottolog's representative point for Sichuan Yi.
